section.heading: Use of minors in crimes of violence

subsection.heading: Definitions.—
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the following definitions shall apply:

paragraph.heading: Crime of violence.—
paragraph.content: The term “crime of violence” has the meaning set forth in section 16.

paragraph.heading: Minor.—
paragraph.content: The term “minor” means a person who has not reached 18 years of age.

paragraph.heading: Uses.—
paragraph.content: The term “uses” means employs, hires, persuades, induces, entices, or coerces.

subsection.heading: Penalties.—
subsection.chapeau: Any person who is 18 years of age or older, who intentionally uses a minor to commit a crime of violence for which such person may be prosecuted in a court of the United States, or to assist in avoiding detection or apprehension for such an offense, shall—

paragraph.content: for the first conviction, be subject to twice the maximum term of imprisonment and twice the maximum fine that would otherwise be authorized for the offense; and
for each subsequent conviction, be subject to 3 times the maximum term of imprisonment and 3 times the maximum fine that would otherwise be authorized for the offense.
